calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-25-2013 Off Calendar

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Deft And Req. For Monetary Sanctions And Pos
Off Calendar Jul-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF SAHIL MANSURI'S MOTION To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Deft And Req. For Monetary Sanctions IS GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff propounded Judicial Council Form Interrogatories- General (DISC-001) to each defendant. Plaintiff checked the box for 19 of these Form Interrogatories in each set. Plaintiff also propounded 16 identical requests for production to each defendant. Each defendant answered the first 16 interrogatories in each set (including all of their subparts), and then objected to the remaining interrogatories and all of the document requests on the ground that, "[t]he propounding party has exceeded the permissible limit of 35 interrogatories." None of the defendants objected to any of the interrogatories on the ground that they contained subparts. This would have been a valid objection to each interrogatory containing subparts. C.C.P. Section 94(a) allows a party in a limited civil case to propound to each adverse party, "any combination of 35 of the following:(1) Interrogatories (with no subparts) . . . Demands to produce documents or things . . . and (3) Requests for admission (with no subparts) . . . ." Use of the Form Interrogatories-General in limited civil litigation violates the rule against propounding interrogatories with subparts in Section 94(a), and the Judicial Council has promulgated Form Interrogatories for use in limited civil cases that do not contain subparts. See Judicial Council Form Interrogatories - Limited Civil Cases (Economic Litigation) form DISC-004. However, by answering without failing to object to the interrogatories containing subparts, the defendants waived their objections. Nonetheless the court will not order the defendants to answer additional interrogatories that violate C.C.P. Section 94(a). Each Defendant shall respond to the 16 requests for production of documents in Plaintiff's Requests for Production - Set One. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Pro Tem Judge: Ernst Halperin; Not reported. =302/JPT
